content:
Incident Report
Page 10 of 11
27, .223 rounds .
The last witness officer we were introduced to was Deputy Casey M. McEathron. I began by taking overall photos of Deputy McEathron. I documented Deputy McEathron's Glock 17 9mm pistol (serial number: BGMB590) . The magazine that was inserted into the handgun contained 17 rounds. As I cleared the handgun, I observed one round extract from the chamber. Detective Sergeant Brockway's had two backup handgun magazines. Both backup handgun magazines contained 17, 9mm rounds each.
After we were done processing all shooting and witness officers, we were told to respond to the scene of the incident.
On Friday, September 04, 2020, at approximately 0000 hours, I arrived at the 3rd Way SE address. The scene was located in an apartment complex parking lot. I observed a deceased male laying in the parking lot. The beige Volkswagen was still running and there were several unmarked vehicles surrounding the Volkswagen. There were several numbered yellow placards on the ground, which were used to identify pieces of evidence.
Detective Sergeant Engelbertson, Detective Brown, and I were tasked with processing and inventorying vehicles of the shooting and witness officers. first vehicle we were assigned was Deputy Munson's patrol vehicle, bearing Washington State license plate XMTC8752C. I was tasked with locating items of interest, Detective Brown was tasked with photographing items of interest and Detective Sergeant Engelbertson was tasked with annotating items of interest. Deputy Brown and I worked together to conduct round counts of the ammunition found inside Deputy Munson's patrol vehicle.
The
We then inventoried a dark blue Dodge Charger, bearing Washington State license plate AXR0804, which was driven by one of the Task Force Officer's. Detective Sergeant Engelbertson, Detective Brown, and I kept the same assignments that we had when processing Deputy Munson's patrol vehicle.
For a detailed description of the items of interest located in each vehicle, please refer Detective Sergeant Engelbertson's report for more information. To see photos of the items of interest located in each vehicle, please refer to Detective Brown's report for more information.
Shortly after processing the second vehicle, we were told we could clear from the scene.
This concludes my report.
I CERTIFY UNDER PENALTY OF PERJURY UNDER THE LAWS OF THE STATE OF WASHINGTON THAT ALL STATEMENTS MADE HEREIN ARE TRUE AND ACCURATE AND THAT I AM ENTERING MY AUTHORIZED USER ID AND PASSWORD TO AUTHENTICATE IT (RCW 9A. 72.085).
Electronically Signed: Yes Signature: Detective Scott L. Robb #2D4 Date: 09/11/2020
Chehalis/Lewis/Washington
Printed: 10:55:37 09/14/20
rplwdir.x1
09/14/20
